rationale: ADDRESSED. The added paragraph in ms-panel-run § Step 0 (both plugins/ and .claude/ copies, verified byte-identical) reads: '`panel create` prints a `panel directory: <dir>` line. Capture that exact path and use it directly for the rest of this skill — the BRIEF stub, the codex prompt files, and the verdict-file existence checks — instead of re-deriving it from the `<spec-dir>/reviews/<panel-slug>/` convention note above.' This is exactly my round-1 ask: the operator now points at the CLI's own authoritative output rather than re-deriving the path from prose. It also explicitly names `panelDirFor`'s non-flat legacy fallback (`<root>/review/<slug>`) and states the reported line always resolves it correctly where the prose convention doesn't — closing the second half of my finding too. No dangling reference introduced: the new paragraph sits cleanly between the co-bump paragraph and the abandon-procedure bullet, doesn't disturb numbering or cross-references. Bonus: runbook.md now enumerates all 5 surviving ms-panel-run sections (my round-1 completeness nit), and the stale `expected-reviewers` § Inputs bullet is replaced with an accurate config-stamping note. Nothing new introduced by this delta breaks operator flow.
findings: Confirmed via `git diff 13788634..17eb5afe`: the fix is additive-only to ms-panel-run (my finding) and ms-panel-tally (R7's finding) plus the runbook doc-sync; no removed or restructured content that could dangle a reference.
